carbon_policy_signal.item: EU tech sovereignty plan disclosure (June 3) expected to signal renewable capex acceleration and green industrial policy alignment; potential MSR intake signal via emissions-intensive data-center power demand disclosure rules.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Sustained renewable buildout and data-center power demand clarity reduces long-term structural fuel-switching carbon intensity, pressuring EUA floor if MSR intake accelerates.

risk_flags: Storage at 39.1% (16th percentile, 14 pp below seasonal) — H2 refill pace will define power curve risk and gas-to-coal fuel-switch economics.
DE Power extended 2.1σ above 50d trend; daily jump 32.7% suggests tactical extraction risk if heat moderates or renewables surge.
Hormuz escalation threat elevates crude and tightens LNG arb window, indirectly lifting EU TTF risk premium despite flat near-term seasonal.
Clean Spark 96th percentile sustainability depends on sustained storage deficit and continued thermal loading — vulnerable to rain, demand softening, or nuclear return.

base.summary: Record heat sustains AC demand and thermal dispatch; storage deficit maintains power premium and fuel-switch backdrop.

downside.summary: Heat moderates mid-week; renewable output surge (wind/solar); storage refill accelerates; Hormuz tensions ease. TTF soft; thermal margin compresses.

upside.summary: Hormuz escalation or crude spike; storage refill delays through June; renewables miss on forecast. TTF rises via LNG arb tightening; thermal stays extended.

themes: Gas fundamentals tight, storage deficit structural
Clean Spark in-the-money; thermal dispatch critical
Geopolitical risk premium via Hormuz and EU weaponization fears
Record heat boosting AC demand and renewable output tension
top_drivers: Clean Spark at 96th percentile driven by storage deficit (39.1%, 14 pp below seasonal) forcing sustained thermal merit-order extension.
Record May heat dome lifting AC peak demand and thermal fleet stress while exposing water cooling constraints; renewable output volatility increases tail risk to power swings.
Hormuz geopolitical escalation and EU fossil-fuel weaponization narrative elevating risk premium on TTF and LNG arb; indirect fuel-switch dynamics support thermal margins.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 96th percentile, storage 14 pp below seasonal, and record heat driving thermal stress create a tight, bullish power regime; Hormuz escalation and EU strategic pivot reinforce geopolitical risk premium on fossil fuels.

watchlist.metric: EU Storage deficit (14 pp below seasonal)
watchlist.why: Refill pace through June-July sets thermal dispatch marginal cost and Cal+1 curve shape; delays risk H2 power floor spike.

watchlist.metric: Hormuz escalation transmission to LNG arb
watchlist.why: If crude spikes >10%, narrowed TTF-HH spread could flip, reducing EU LNG inflows and tightening gas supply signal in July–Aug wind drought scenarios.

watchlist.metric: Heat dome duration and renewable forecast revision
watchlist.why: Record May temperatures driving peak AC; if sustained into June and renewables underperform expectations, thermal merit-order extension deepens.

watchlist.metric: EU data center power demand disclosure (June 3 tech sovereignty plan)
watchlist.why: Potential long-term Cal+1 and Cal+2 demand uplift; clarifies ES capex outlook and grid reinforcement needs affecting forward hedges.

watchlist_dated.name: EU tech sovereignty plan disclosure
watchlist_dated.why: May signal renewable capex acceleration and data-center power demand growth, supporting Cal+1 bull narrative.

watchlist_dated.name: COP31 Turkey host role coordination
watchlist_dated.why: Cyprus exclusion risk threatens climate policy alignment; uncertainty could shift EU carbon policy trajectory and ETS messaging.
